lines.line: # Funding for Gem County Coroner's Office
> John Bucks explains the way he leveraged the small budget of the Gem County Coroner's Office  during the pandemic. He describes cultivating good working relationships with other elected officials.
**budget, grants, personal protective equipment, County Commissioners**

metadata.title: Covid and the Elderly
metadata.synopsis: John Buck concludes his oral history interview by sharing his experience dealing with an elderly parent who contracted Covid-19. He mentions the treatment his mother obtained, which assisted in saving her life.

children.text: > John Buck finishes sharing how he assisted with getting a law passed that funded professional development for county Coroner's in the state of Idaho.
**education, professional development, death certificate, Brad Little**
# Funding for Gem County Coroner's Office
> John Bucks explains the way he leveraged the small budget of the Gem County Coroner's Office  during the pandemic. He describes cultivating good working relationships with other elected officials.
**budget, grants, personal protective equipment, County Commissioners**
> John Bucks describes the changes that occurred in Gem County during the pandemic. He references reduced hours, restaurants, grocery shopping, and church attendance.

> John Buck concludes his oral history interview by sharing his experience dealing with an elderly parent who contracted Covid-19. He mentions the treatment his mother obtained, which assisted in saving her life.

story.description: John Buck shares his COVID experiences as a funeral director, Gem County Coroner, and a member of the Emmett community.
story.title: Buck_John_OH2773b COVID-19 Oral History
